en: Then Job answered and said:
zh: 約伯回答說：

en: "Truly I know that it is so: But how can a man be in the right before God?
zh: “我實在知道是這樣， 但是，人在 神面前怎能算為公義呢？

en: If one wished to contend with him, one could not answer him once in a thousand times.
zh: 人若願意與他辯論， 連千分之一也答不出來。

en: He is wise in heart and mighty in strength- who has hardened himself against him, and succeeded?-
zh: 他心裡有智慧，而且有極大的能力， 有誰對他剛硬而平安無事呢？

en: he who removes mountains, and they know it not, when he overturns them in his anger,
zh: 他在怒中移山翻岡； 山岡卻不知道；

en: who shakes the earth out of its place, and its pillars tremble;
zh: 他使大地震動離開本處， 大地的柱子就搖撼；

en: who commands the sun, and it does not rise; who seals up the stars;
zh: 他吩咐太陽，太陽就不上升， 又封閉眾星；

en: who alone stretched out the heavens and trampled the waves of the sea;
zh: 他獨自鋪開蒼天， 步行在海浪之上；

en: who made the Bear and Orion, the Pleiades and the chambers of the south;
zh: 他造北斗與參星， 昴星和南方的星座；

en: who does great things beyond searching out, and marvelous things beyond number.
zh: 他所行的大事無法測度， 所行的奇事不可勝數。

en: Behold, he passes by me, and I see him not; he moves on, but I do not perceive him.
zh: 他行過我身邊，我卻看不見； 他掠過去，我竟不覺察。

en: Behold, he snatches away; who can turn him back? Who will say to him, 'What are you doing?'
zh: 他奪取，誰能攔阻他？ 誰敢問他：‘你幹甚麼？’

en: "God will not turn back his anger; beneath him bowed the helpers of Rahab.
zh: 神必不抑制他的怒氣， 海怪拉哈伯的助手都俯伏在他以下。

en: How then can I answer him, choosing my words with him?
zh: 何況我呢？我怎敢回答他， 措辭與他辯論呢？

en: Though I am in the right, I cannot answer him; I must appeal for mercy to my accuser.
zh: 即使我有理，也不敢回答， 只向那審判我的求憐憫；

en: If I summoned him and he answered me, I would not believe that he was listening to my voice.
zh: 即使我呼求，他也回答我， 我還是不信他會垂聽我的聲音。

en: For he crushes me with a tempest and multiplies my wounds without cause;
zh: 他用暴風傷害我， 無緣無故加添我的創傷。

en: he will not let me get my breath, but fills me with bitterness.
zh: 他不讓我喘一口氣， 卻使我飽嘗苦楚。

en: If it is a contest of strength, behold, he is mighty! If it is a matter of justice, who can summon him?
zh: 若論力量，他多麼強大， 若論訴訟，他說：‘誰能把我傳來？’

en: Though I am in the right, my own mouth would condemn me; though I am blameless, he would prove me perverse.
zh: 即使我有理，我的口還是定我有罪； 即使我完全，我的口還是判我乖謬。

en: I am blameless; I regard not myself; I loathe my life.
zh: 我雖然完全，卻不顧我自己， 倒厭惡我的生命。

en: It is all one; therefore I say, He destroys both the blameless and the wicked.
zh: 所以我說，善惡都是一樣， 完全人和惡人，他都滅盡。

en: When disaster brings sudden death, he mocks at the calamity of the innocent.
zh: 災禍忽然把人殺害的時候， 他就必嘲笑無辜人的遭遇。

en: The earth is given into the hand of the wicked; he covers the faces of its judges- if it is not he, who then is it?
zh: 全世界交在惡人的手中， 他蒙蔽世上審判官的臉， 如果不是他，那麼是誰呢？

en: "My days are swifter than a runner; they flee away; they see no good.
zh: 我的日子過得比信差還快， 飛快逝去，不見福樂。

en: They go by like skiffs of reed, like an eagle swooping on the prey.
zh: 我的日子消逝有如快船， 好像俯衝猛撲食物的鷹。

en: If I say, 'I will forget my complaint, I will put off my sad face, and be of good cheer,'
zh: 我若說：‘我要忘記我的苦情， 要除去愁容，面露喜樂。’

en: I become afraid of all my suffering, for I know you will not hold me innocent.
zh: 我就懼怕我的一切痛苦， 因為我知道你必不以我為無辜。

en: I shall be condemned; why then do I labor in vain?
zh: 我既然被定為有罪， 又何必徒然勞苦呢？

en: If I wash myself with snow and cleanse my hands with lye,
zh: 我若用雪水洗淨我的身， 又用鹼水潔淨我的手，

en: yet you will plunge me into a pit, and my own clothes will abhor me.
zh: 你還是把我扔入坑中， 連我的衣服也憎惡我。

en: For he is not a man, as I am, that I might answer him, that we should come to trial together.
zh: 他不像我是個人， 使我可以答他，讓我們一起對簿公堂。

en: There is no arbiter between us, who might lay his hand on us both.
zh: 我倆之間並沒有仲裁者， 能夠按手在我們雙方身上。

en: Let him take his rod away from me, and let not dread of him terrify me.
zh: 願他使他的刑杖離開我， 願他可畏的威嚴不驚嚇我，

en: Then I would speak without fear of him, for I am not so in myself.
zh: 我就說話，也不怕他， 因為我本身並不是這種人。”
